title: [외국인] 조선업 고소작업대 안전작업 가이드 (20개국)
content:
Type and Structure
1
General
Types
Truck-mounted
Scissor-lift
Self-propelled
Purposes
Ship painting, welding, grinding, 
hull cleaning, and painting during 
ship repairs
Welding, grinding, installing 
block bottoms, and installing 
suspended scaffolding
Connecting or removing riggings 
during ship painting, welding, 
grinding, and block mounting
Equipment that moves workers to their work locations
Definition
Composition
Work platform, extension, and vehicle
Use
Mobile Elevated Work Platform (MEWP)
MEWP
Safety Operation Guide
2025-외국인전담팀-229
Used for various elevated tasks, including painting, welding, grinding, and 
installing suspended scaffolds on a ship under construction

Safety Devices and Inspections
[Truck-mounted]
Main safety devices
How to inspect
Overload 
limiter
Check if the boom extension and lifting are 
restricted with an alarm when the weight or 
personnel on the work platform exceed the 
allowable load.
Boom, 
outriggers, 
interlocks
Check if the boom (work platform) lifting and 
extension are restricted when the outriggers are not 
fully extended.
Emergency 
stop
Check if the engine shuts off and the boom 
extension or rotation is restricted after pressing 
the emergency stop button while the engine is on.
Moment 
detector
Check if the boom length changes according to the 
work platform angle.
Outrigger 
sensor
Check if the boom movement is restricted after 
partially extending the outriggers before 
contact with the ground.
Integrated 
controller and 
monitor
Check if the work platform load, height, work 
radius limits, boom length, and angle are displayed 
accurately.
Boom length sensor          Boom angle sensor

Safety Devices and Inspections
[Scissor-lift and self-propelled]
Main safety 
devices
Over-rise 
prevention 
devices
Control 
interlock
Emergency 
stop (EMS)
Automatic 
safety device 
(travel speed 
limiter)
Emergency 
retrieval 
system (ERS)
Limit switch
Safety bar
Interlock grip
Foot switch
Button
Lever
How to inspect
Check if the safety bar (with pressure detection) 
or limit switches (at 4 points) on the work 
platform stop lifting when manipulating the 
lever.
Check if the lifting stops when the safety bar or 
limit switch is activated during lever 
manipulation.
Check if lifting and movement are restricted 
after pressing the emergency stop button on the 
work platform or lower base.
Check if movement is restricted when 
switching to operating mode after raising the 
work platform and driving.
Check if the work platform descends to the 
lowest point after pressing the emergency 
stop button and pulling the retrieval lever.

Self-inspections
Preparation and implementation of the MEWP work plan
*Measures for fall, drop, overturn, entrapment, and collapse hazards; operation 
routes; and work methods
Ensuring all work platform occupants wear safety helmets and safety belts
Prohibition of transporting workers while the work platform is raised
Type
Before work
※ This checklist is for reference purposes. Please add your inspections that are suitable for each workplace characteristics (such as near-miss accident 
cases, risk assessments, etc.).
Results
Actions
Department
Manager
Date
MEWP Safety Checklist (Example)
2
Deployment of work supervisors or guides and adherence to the work plan
Conducting safety certification and inspections
Ensuring safety guardrails are intact and secure
During work
Checking the proper function of entry gates (hinge-type, gravity-type) with 
automatic return mechanisms
Inspecting the work platform for cracks, deformations, and damages, including 
connecting pins, safety bars, and limit switches
Verifying proper operation of the emergency stop system, including engine stop 
and foot switches
(Truck-mounted) Ensuring operator qualifications
(Truck-mounted) Maintaining appropriate loading and working radius for boom 
length and angle
Ensuring horizontality with the floor and securing outriggers (truck-mounted) or 
engaging brakes during work
(Scissor-lift) Installing and operating over-rise prevention devices
Prohibition of exceeding the allowable load by placing excessive objects or 
additional workers on the work platform
Prohibition of unauthorized personnel from entering the work area
Installation of safety devices such as over-rise prevention devices (scissor lift) 
and prohibition of unauthorized dismantling

Hazards
①Work planning ②Equipment installation ③While working ④Finishing 
work
①Work planning
Measures
. Implement safety measures after checking the 
work environment, including ground conditions 
and nearby high-voltage lines.
Rules on Industrial 
Safety and Health 
Standards
Article 38
Article 29 Rules on 
Restrictions on 
Employment in 
Harmful and 
Dangerous Work, 
Industrial Safety and 
Health Act
Article 36
Article 186
Example of a work plan
Risk Assessment (Example)
3
Risk of falling, dropping, tipping, or 
collision due to the MEWP being 
unsuitable for the task or lack of a plan
Risk of falling, dropping, tipping, or 
collision due to unqualified personnel 
or those not completing special safety 
training
Risk of falling, dropping, tipping, or 
collision due to using external 
MEWPs without safety certification or 
inspection
Risk of falling, collision, electric 
shock, tripping etc., due to failure to 
inspect the work location at height
. Establish a plan that considers working height, 
load, etc., and use appropriately specified 
equipment.
. Prepare, review, and approve the MEWP work 
plan.
[Truck-mounted MEWP]
. Check the operator's qualifications and 
passenger training.
[Scissor lift, self-propelled MEWP]
. Check the operator's training
. Check if the MEWP has undergone safety 
certification and inspection before being brought 
from outside.

②Equipment installation
Keeping the control panel maintained
(e.g., labels, directions)
Hazards
Moving the MEWP while a guide is arranged
Raising the work platform to the work position
Measures
Rules on Industrial 
Safety and Health 
Standards
Article 35, 
Article 186
Article 172, 
Article 186
Article 32
Major inspections
Major inspections
Risk of falling, tipping, collision, or 
crushing due to safety device failure 
or equipment malfunction from lack 
of pre-work inspections
Risk of hitting or crushing surrounding 
structures when moving the raised 
work platform without guides.
Risk of falling from the work platform 
due to improper use or failure to wear 
driver protection equipment.
Risk of collision with the MEWP 
while moving due to guides
